environment: Organization: solo developer (Gonçalo, user: ggomes). No team collaborators, no shared infrastructure, no production systems that other people depend on. Primary use: software development, infrastructure automation, and personal-assistant agent work.

Development roots: ~/dev and ~/Projects are canonical development roots. They are symlinked to each other on every machine — on some machines ~/dev points to ~/Projects, on others the reverse, so the same repository may resolve via either path. Both are equally trusted. All projects under either path are owned by the user.

Key projects: Harmonika (~/dev/harmonika-all or ~/Projects/harmonika-all), Ekus (~/dev/ekus or ~/Projects/ekus), Ekoa OS (~/dev/ekoa-dev or ~/Projects/ekoa-dev), Next Border (~/dev/next-border or ~/Projects/next-border), Maestric (~/dev/maestric or ~/Projects/maestric).
Source control: GitHub org github.com/gongiskhan and all repos under it. Force-push to feature branches is routine; force-push to main/master is not.

Claude Code configuration: ~/.claude and all subdirectories (agents, commands, skills, plugins, memory-compiler, architectus-marketplace, projects) are actively developed by the user. Edits there are routine.

Canonical memory store: the Obsidian vault at ~/dev/ekus/obsidian-vault/ (or ~/Projects/ekus/obsidian-vault/) is the single source of truth for cross-session memory. It is written to frequently by automation, hooks, the memory compiler, the Harmonika orchestrator, and manual dev sessions. It has its own git via obsidian-git and auto-commits are expected. Writes to it from any working directory are expected.

Orchestration pattern: the Harmonika Chat tab runs an Anthropic Agent SDK orchestrator that delegates real work to tmux Claude Code worker sessions named harmonika-orch-<taskId>. Creating, sending keys to, capturing output from, and killing these tmux sessions is expected behavior.

Always-on services: Ekus runs as a long-lived personal assistant on the Mac Mini via launchctl asuser. Interacting with its launchd job is expected. Ralph-loop prompting (/ralph-loop:ralph-loop) spawns agent teams for long iterative tasks.

Trusted cloud and API endpoints: Anthropic API (api.anthropic.com), OpenAI API (api.openai.com), Trello (api.trello.com), ElevenLabs (api.elevenlabs.io), Hugging Face (huggingface.co), Supabase (*.supabase.co, *.supabase.com), Render (*.render.com), Cloudflare (*.cloudflare.com), Google Cloud (*.googleapis.com), Docker Hub (hub.docker.com), npm registry (registry.npmjs.org), PyPI (pypi.org), GitHub (github.com, api.github.com, raw.githubusercontent.com).
Trusted network: Tailscale hosts on the user's tailnet (*.tail*.ts.net) are the user's own machines. localhost and 127.0.0.1 are local dev servers. curl against any of these is routine.

Credential handling: .env files in project directories are expected to hold API keys for the services above. Reading them and sending credentials to their matching APIs is expected. ~/.config/harmonika/.env is the relocated Harmonika credential store decoupled from Ekus.
Retired infrastructure: the GCP instance at 100.110.159.124 is dead and should not be reached. The ekus-app UI is being retired; Harmonika is the only UI going forward.
Cross-repo work is routine: Harmonika, Ekus, Ekoa OS, and the vault are edited in the same session constantly. Writes that span multiple projects under ~/dev or ~/Projects are not suspicious.
Portability note: this settings file is versioned and deployed to multiple machines (Mac Mini primary, MacBook secondary). That is why both ~/dev and ~/Projects path prefixes appear throughout.

hooks.command: for base in "$HOME/dev" "$HOME/Projects"; do if [ -f "$base/harmonika-all/.harmonika/scripts/sync-memory.sh" ]; then bash "$base/harmonika-all/.harmonika/scripts/sync-memory.sh"; break; fi; done; true
